name: Graphics Driver
description: Software component that abstracts GPU hardware and exposes standardized interfaces to the operating system and user-space applications for rendering, display output management, compute workloads, and hardware-accelerated media processing. Operates across both kernel-mode (KMD) and user-mode (UMD) driver components within the OS graphics subsystem.
purpose: Provision of hardware abstraction for GPU rendering pipelines, display adapter enumeration and configuration, graphics API translation, hardware-accelerated compute offload, and display output management including multi-monitor topology and refresh rate control.

name: Audio Driver
description: Software component that abstracts audio hardware including HD Audio controllers, USB audio devices, and digital signal processors, exposing standardized interfaces to the OS audio subsystem for audio rendering, capture, mixing, and routing. Operates across kernel-mode bus and codec drivers and user-space audio engine components.
purpose: Hardware abstraction for audio rendering and capture pipelines, audio device enumeration, sample rate and bit-depth negotiation, hardware mixing offload, acoustic signal processing, and audio endpoint routing within the OS audio subsystem.

name: Chipset Driver
description: Foundational platform driver package that enables the operating system to correctly identify, enumerate, and communicate with the motherboard chipset components including PCIe root complexes, system bus controllers, SMBus, GPIO, UART, I2C, SPI interfaces, and power management controllers. Provides the platform abstraction layer upon which all other device drivers depend.
purpose: Provision of OS-level enumeration and communication support for chipset subsystems including PCIe topology, system bus controllers, I/O hub interfaces, power management integration, and platform device identification required by dependent drivers.

name: Network Driver
description: Software component that abstracts network interface controller hardware and exposes a standardized interface to the operating system network stack for wired Ethernet, wireless LAN, Bluetooth, and mobile broadband connectivity. Operates within the OS networking subsystem handling packet transmission, reception, hardware offload, and power management for network adapters.
purpose: Hardware abstraction for network packet transmission and reception, NIC feature negotiation, hardware offload configuration, power management of network adapters, wireless association management, and integration with OS network stack protocols.

name: Storage Driver
description: Software component that abstracts storage controller and device hardware, providing the operating system storage stack with standardized interfaces for block device enumeration, I/O request processing, command translation, and storage-specific feature management including hardware encryption, power states, and error recovery across NVMe, SATA, SAS, USB, and SD/MMC interfaces.
purpose: Hardware abstraction for storage device I/O operations, storage controller enumeration, block device management, command set translation, hardware encryption feature integration, power state management, and error recovery handling within the OS storage stack.

name: Driver Pack
description: A distribution package that bundles multiple device drivers — typically including network, chipset, graphics, audio, and storage drivers — into a single versioned release for deployment across managed endpoint fleets. Driver Packs are used to standardize driver baselines, simplify OS deployment, and enforce driver compatibility across heterogeneous hardware configurations. Subject to OS compatibility requirements and minimum version constraints from platform advisories.
purpose: Provision of a unified, versioned driver distribution artifact that standardizes driver baselines across managed endpoint fleets, supporting OS deployment workflows, compatibility validation, and systematic driver lifecycle management.

provenance.reason: Discovered during Layer 3 compatibility entity resolution — required for driver/OS compatibility rules (RCAND-000360, RCAND-000369, RCAND-000371, RCAND-000375, RCAND-000376, RCAND-000381, RCAND-000387, RCAND-000393, RCAND-000399, RCAND-000400). Versions observed: 12.3.0, 12.4.0, 12.4.2, 12.5.0.

review_notes: Added in Layer 3 entity resolution phase. Represents a multi-driver distribution bundle, distinct from individual driver type entities (DRV-001 through DRV-008). Registered at DRV-009 following existing Driver namespace sequence.

name: Platform Driver Pack
description: A hardware-platform-specific variant of a driver distribution bundle, optimized and validated for a specific device family or hardware platform (e.g., workstation class hardware). Contains platform-specific driver configurations and hardware dependencies not present in general Driver Pack releases. Subject to hardware-model-specific version requirements from platform advisories.
purpose: Provision of a platform-optimized, versioned driver distribution artifact targeted at specific hardware device families, supporting validated driver configurations for platform-specific hardware components including NICs, storage controllers, and platform management interfaces.

provenance.reason: Discovered during Layer 3 compatibility entity resolution — used in EdgeStation-specific and OS compatibility contexts (RCAND-000364, RCAND-000365, RCAND-000367, RCAND-000400). Version observed: 12.5.0.

review_notes: Added in Layer 3 entity resolution phase. Distinct from Driver Pack (DRV-009) — used in hardware-model-specific and EdgeStation workstation contexts. Registered at DRV-010 following existing Driver namespace sequence. Relationship to DRV-009 should be evaluated for IS_A or PART_OF in Phase 4 rule production.

name: Driver Signing
description: Process of digitally signing driver packages to ensure their authenticity and integrity.
purpose: Verify that driver packages haven't been tampered with and come from trusted sources.

name: WDDM
description: Windows Display Driver Model, a graphics driver architecture for Windows.
purpose: Provide a standardized graphics driver architecture for Windows.

name: NDIS
description: Network Driver Interface Specification, a network driver architecture for Windows.
purpose: Provide a standardized network driver architecture for Windows.
